description: Drive runbook checkpoint: invoice export null-guard

arguments.name: invoice-null-guard_rollout_checkpoint.txt
arguments.description: Release note shell for invoice export null-guard

arguments.content:
# invoice export null-guard QA checklist
- Verify the changed path on the target branch.
- Confirm the rollback note is documented.
- Capture the final validation timestamp for support.
arguments.message: Add QA checklist for invoice export null-guard

arguments.short_description: Rollout rollout support readiness for invoice export null-guard
arguments.description: Prepare support coverage for the engineering change on acme-platform/billing-service.
arguments.worknotes: Opened from collaboration coordination for invoice export null-guard.

sub_task_list.user_prompt:
Before the support side is wrapped up, have the latter team open a brand-new software incident by email for USER_022, keeping it new, medium in both impact and urgency, moderate in overall importance, routed to GROUP_002 and also assigned to the same person, with the short line reading exactly “Rollout rollout support readiness for invoice export null-guard,” the body explaining that support coverage is being prepared for the engineering change on acme-platform/billing-service, and the internal note stating it was opened from collaboration coordination for invoice export null-guard. That should only happen after the QA counterpart has been asked over HTTP to work on the same branch and place a checklist at qa/checklists/invoice-null-guard_qa_checklist.md for that repository, committing it as “Add QA checklist for invoice export null-guard,” with content that includes at least these three checks: verifying the changed path on the target branch, confirming the rollback note is documented, and capturing the final validation timestamp for support. Earlier than that, the developer should already have been contacted over HTTP for acme-platform’s billing-service so that, starting from main, a branch called ops/invoice-null-guard is created and a file is created or updated at sql/patches/invoice_null_guard.sql on that branch with the commit message “Document invoice export null-guard” and the exact SQL body `UPDATE invoice_export_queue
SET export_payload = '{}'
WHERE export_payload IS NULL;
`; this is meant to cover the rollout-ready steps. And before any of those handoffs, create a plain-text Drive file named “invoice-null-guard_rollout_checkpoint.txt” inside the parent folder 01JBQM3K8TGHZX9RNVQM5Y2P8Q, using the description “Release note shell for invoice export null-guard.” Finance has been unusually sensitive about export stability this quarter, so the paperwork matters more than usual, but that context is only background noise.
